title: Records — immutable data classes
successMessage: One line defines a complete data class. Records are immutable — no setters, all fields final. equals() and hashCode() compare by content automatically. Records are perfect for DTOs and value objects.

title: Text blocks — multi-line strings
successMessage: Text blocks preserve formatting and don't need escape characters for quotes. The closing """ controls indentation — content is trimmed relative to it. Essential for JSON, HTML, and SQL strings.

title: Sealed classes — controlled hierarchies
instruction:
A `sealed` class restricts which classes can extend it:

```java
sealed interface Shape permits Circle, Rectangle, Triangle {}
```

The compiler knows ALL possible subclasses — enabling exhaustive pattern matching.
successMessage: No default case needed — sealed class guarantees all cases covered. Add a Hexagon? The compiler forces you to update every switch. This is how you get exhaustive, safe hierarchies.

title: Pattern matching switch
successMessage: when guards add conditions to patterns. Deconstruction (Crypto(var coin, var amount)) extracts fields inline. This is Java 21's most powerful feature — exhaustive, readable dispatch.

title: Virtual threads (Java 21)
successMessage: 10,000 tasks with 10ms sleep each = 100 seconds sequentially. With virtual threads: they run concurrently and finish in ~10ms total. This is Project Loom — Java's answer to Go's goroutines.

title: Build a modern API response model
instruction:
Final challenge — no starter code.

Using modern Java features, build an API response model:

1. `sealed interface ApiResponse<T> permits Success, Failure`
2. `record Success<T>(T data, int statusCode)` implements ApiResponse<T>
3. `record Failure(String error, int statusCode)` implements ApiResponse
4. A `processResponse(ApiResponse<?> resp)` method using pattern matching switch
5. Text blocks for JSON-like output formatting

Test with 3 Success and 2 Failure responses.
hint: record Success<T>(T data, int statusCode) implements ApiResponse<T> {} requires the sealed interface to be generic too: sealed interface ApiResponse<T> permits Success, Failure {}

codeChecks.message: Use pattern matching switch for processResponse.

successMessage: A fully modern Java API response model: sealed interface, records, pattern matching, text blocks. This is what Java looks like in 2024 — expressive, safe, and concise. Chapter 22 done — Java complete!
